vix.note: VIX elevated; intraday spike to 23.24 before settling at 19.87 as hawkish repricing follows blowout May jobs report

macro: Jobs shock reignites Fed hike bets, pressuring tech and semis. U.S. May payrolls surged to 172,000 - nearly double the 88,000 consensus - pushing market-implied odds of at least one Fed hike above 60% by year-end. The Nasdaq fell 4% and S&P 500 dropped 2.6% on June 5 (worst session in over a year); markets remain under pressure on June 9 with VIX at 19.87 (intraday high 23.24). Broadcom's Q3 AI chip guidance miss ($16B vs. $17.2B estimated) adds sector-specific headwinds; Wednesday's May CPI print is the next major catalyst.
action: Hold existing positions and avoid adding to high-multiple AI names (NVDA, AVGO) ahead of Wednesday CPI; GOLD at $4,283 offers relative safe-haven exposure while rate-hike risk persists.

notes.NVDA: NVDA held near key support at $208 during Tuesday's continued sell-off. Elon Musk's claim of building chips 2 to 3 times better than Nvidia at 10 percent of the cost is an unverified speculative risk - not an immediate threat - but adds overhang to an already pressured name.
notes.AVGO: AVGO is down roughly 11 percent since the June 3 earnings report where Q3 AI chip guidance ($16B vs. $17.2B estimated) disappointed despite a revenue beat. Recovery attempts remain fragile; next earnings Sept 3, 2026.
notes.INTC: INTC faces compounding headwinds: Nvidia expanding into client CPUs threatens Intel's core x86 franchise on top of existing data-center share pressure. Musk chip claim, if real, would be a further longer-term headwind.

long.h: Nvidia tumbles to key support level amid continued market sell-off
long.p: NVDA held near a critical technical support zone as AI and semiconductor names remained under pressure from hawkish jobs data and rate-hike bets. A decisive bounce from support would be a bullish signal.

long.h: Elon Musk claims chip 2-3x better than Nvidia at 10 percent of the cost - is he bluffing?
long.p: Musk told Baron Capital founder Ron Baron that Tesla is building AI chips far superior to Nvidia at a fraction of the cost. Analysts are skeptical given no proof of concept, but the headline adds competitive overhang to NVDA and INTC.

long.h: Broadcom stock down more than 10 percent after investor update - what happened?
long.p: AVGO's Q3 AI chip revenue guidance of $16B came in below the $17.2B analyst estimate despite strong Q2 results (revenue up 48% YoY). The market sold the guidance gap; next earnings Sept 3, EPS estimate $3.21.

long.h: Stock markets sell off - is Nvidia an excellent buy on the dip?
long.p: Semiconductor stocks fell after the May jobs report showed the economy adding nearly double the expected jobs, reinforcing hawkish Fed expectations. Analysts debate whether the AI structural story justifies buying NVDA into weakness.

long.h: Nasdaq plunges 4%, S&P 500 sinks as jobs report fuels Fed hike bets and AI trade stalls
long.p: May payrolls: 172,000 vs. 88,000 expected. Market-implied Fed hike odds rose above 60%. Nasdaq suffered its worst day in over a year on June 5; follow-through selling continued June 9 with VIX at 19.87.

short.h: Bitcoin slides to $62,044 as risk-off tone from hawkish jobs data hits crypto
short.p: BTC fell from $63,063 to $62,044 (-1.6%) on June 9 as the broader risk-off sell-off following the May jobs shock weighed on crypto alongside equities.

short.h: BTC at a CPI crossroads: rally toward $70k or slide back to $60k
short.p: The rebound from the $60k region eased selling pressure, but Wednesday's CPI may decide whether buyers can build momentum or whether support gives way again.

short.h: Coinbase strategist: institutions are buying BTC near $60-65k, ETF and corporate demand firm
short.p: Large investors prefer accumulating Bitcoin in weakness rather than chasing highs, suggesting the recent drawdown has not scared off institutional buyers.

short.h: Arca rejects Saylor AI-rotation theory, blames Strategy forced sale for last week's BTC crash
short.p: The debate over the drawdown driver matters: a one-off forced sale is far less bearish for BTC than a structural rotation out of crypto into AI equities.

short.h: Hayes warns higher oil could push Trump into anti-AI rhetoric, hitting stocks and Bitcoin together
short.p: A scenario where elevated crude pressures the administration to turn on the AI trade is a reminder that BTC and tech equities remain correlated risk assets in this regime.
